item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Type 'cookie,' you idiot.

— Hackers

Pretty good, although I imagine they would have a bit more consternation about doing something as seemingly dumb as just making a bot named 'Playdough Mouse.' Still, +1 for me.

item.title: Who will keep the cats in check?
item.content:encoded:
Then what will keep the cats in check?

Anyone who played Dwarf Forteress a few times sees where this is going…

Also who will keep in check what keeps cats in check?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple, weird, and creative.

Good stuff as always, Dune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite interesting. I must say, to be honest, that I like this more than I did the other one you posted earlier this week (not to say that that one's bad!). This seems to capture what made SCPs so fun and interesting to me when I was just getting into the site: the tests, the intriguing containment, the fact that the origin of the main anomaly isn't explicitly stated… all this adds up to make this a fresh, nice article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this article; looking forward to the follow-up when the cats start taking over and they need to bring in the digital dogs.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main issue with this is that this isn't really that anomalous. In real life, there are many computer viruses that do what this SCP does. Even then, the core idea is close to SCP-3078.

The primary deal breaker for me is Incident Report 5124-2. It's not entirely necessary; most of the information we need is already contained in the tables adjacent to the addendum. In addition, the tone is jarring in comparison to the first incident log, as well as the rest of the SCP.

There were parts of this I liked, but unfortunately I have to -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something minimalist and short. I'm interested to see how people respond to this one! Thanks to plaguepjp, ralliston, and redofpaw for looking over this, as well as croq for creating the flax code.
